members.name: Bind
members.signature: public int Bind(int port, String host)
members.summary: Opens the TCP socket, and binds it to the specified local address. This method is generally not needed, and only used to force the subsequent call to [method connect_to_host] to use the specified [param host] and [param port] as source address. This can be desired in some NAT punchthrough techniques, or when forcing the source network interface.

members.name: ConnectToHost
members.signature: public int ConnectToHost(String host, int port)
members.summary: Connects to the specified [code]host:port[/code] pair. A hostname will be resolved if valid. Returns [constant OK] on success.

members.name: GetConnectedHost
members.summary: Returns the IP of this peer.

members.name: GetConnectedPort
members.summary: Returns the port of this peer.

members.name: GetLocalPort
members.summary: Returns the local port to which this peer is bound.

members.name: SetNoDelay
members.summary: If [param enabled] is [code]true[/code], packets will be sent immediately. If [param enabled] is [code]false[/code] (the default), packet transfers will be delayed and combined using [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagle%27s_algorithm]Nagle's algorithm[/url]. [b]Note:[/b] It's recommended to leave this disabled for applications that send large packets or need to transfer a lot of data, as enabling this can decrease the total available bandwidth.
